vulnerability.title: The Chronyd service is enabled
vulnerability.description: chrony is a daemon which implements the Network Time Protocol (NTP) is designed to synchronize system clocks across a variety of systems and use a source that is highly accurate. More information on chrony can be found at https://chrony-project.org/. Chrony can be configured to be a client and/or a server. To enable Chronyd service, you can run: # systemctl enable chronyd.service This recommendation only applies if chrony is in use on the system.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q chrony; }; then SYSTEMCTL_EXEC='/usr/bin/systemctl' "$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" unmask 'chronyd.service' if [[ $("$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" is-system-running) != "offline" ]]; then "$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" start 'chronyd.service' fi "$SYSTEMCTL_EXEC" enable 'chronyd.service' else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Enabling the chronyd service is a standard configuration change that does not affect authentication, authorization, or critical system services. It follows typical STIG hardening patterns for service enablement and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.

llm_verdict.message: The remediation steps have been successfully applied. The Chronyd service is now enabled and scheduled to start on the next boot. The service will also be unmasked and started immediately on the current run if possible. No further actions are required.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan will effectively enable chronyd when it is present, addressing the identified vulnerability with no significant security risk. Minor improvements suggested for robustness.
concerns: Enabling chronyd when it is not installed will produce a harmless error and may clutter logs. The plan does not check if the system is online before attempting to start the service, which could result in unnecessary start attempts during offline periods.
suggested_improvements: Check for chrony installation before running enable/start commands; skip the enable step if chrony is absent to avoid error messages. Verify system is online (e.g., via "systemctl is-system-running") before starting chronyd to reduce unnecessary operations. Add error handling for the systemctl commands to ensure failures are logged clearly.
